tasks_completed.notes: Users table with auto-trigger for profile creation on auth signup. Includes role enum (customer, admin, editor, viewer).
RLS policies optimized for auth.uid() performance. Users see own profile, admins see all. Helper functions: is_admin(), current_user_role().
SSR middleware with session refresh, route protection. Admin routes require admin role. Redirects unauthenticated users to login.
All routes use Zod validation, proper error codes per _config/api-conventions.json. Signup creates user with role metadata, trigger auto-creates profile.
Functional React component with form state, API integration, toast notifications. Validates admin role before redirect.

technical_decisions.decision: Use auth.uid() in RLS policies instead of role subqueries
technical_decisions.reason: Research shows 5-10ms performance improvement per query
technical_decisions.tradeoffs: Slightly more complex admin checks, but faster for common case

technical_decisions.decision: Trigger-based profile creation instead of API-side
technical_decisions.reason: Guarantees profile exists even if signup API bypassed
technical_decisions.tradeoffs: Harder to debug, but more reliable

technical_decisions.decision: Separate admin login page vs modal
technical_decisions.reason: Clear separation of concerns, better UX for admin flow

notes_for_next_agent.beta: Auth system ready for product catalog integration. Use /api/auth/login to test authentication flow. Users table has roles - products/categories can use public_read_admin_write pattern from _config/api-conventions.json.
notes_for_next_agent.gamma: CI/CD should include migration execution: supabase db push for alpha migrations. RLS tests require Supabase service role key in test environment.

commit_message: Alpha: Auth system scaffold - users, RLS, middleware, API routes, login page
